narrative:
Sunshine gives way to increasing cloudiness today, and there will be a few afternoon showers entering our weekend weather picture. Tonight, temperatures remain nearly steady and mild with a few showers, and areas of thick Blue Ridge fog. Showers Monday morning (with a thunderstorm possible) will give way to falling afternoon temperatures with rain changing to snow showers. Snow showers and flurries continue into Tuesday morning with dusting-level accumulations for the Blue Ridge, and light accumulations likely for western areas.

The "Mix and Match" Special Offer continues on all the Snowman O'Mometer Pint Glass and Mug (or you can long for a 5 Golfball Day with that pint glass as well). Buy one, and get another drinkware item at 40% off.

Spring Back To Winter

Last Updated Sun March 15 at 10:53AM by Scott Stephens

Sunshine gives way to increasing cloudiness today, and there will be a few afternoon showers entering our weekend weather picture. Tonight, temperatures remain nearly steady and mild with a few showers, and areas of thick Blue Ridge fog. Showers Monday morning (with a thunderstorm possible) will give way to falling afternoon temperatures with rain changing to snow showers. Snow showers and flurries continue into Tuesday morning with dusting-level accumulations for the Blue Ridge, and light accumulations likely for western areas.

The "Mix and Match" Special Offer continues on all the Snowman O'Mometer Pint Glass and Mug (or you can long for a 5 Golfball Day with that pint glass as well). Buy one, and get another drinkware item at 40% off.

Forecast Discussion

A warm front is lifting northward across western North Carolina for the Ides of March. That will promote an increase in low cloudiness today, along with a few afternoon showers. Temperatures tonight will be nearly steady, or perhaps even rising in a few locations as a few showers continue.

A strong cold front arrives around midday on Monday. Showers and perhaps even thunderstorms will accompany and precede the cold front. There will be a heightened risk for severe weather, particularly east of the mountains on Monday.

Once the front gets through, falling temperatures will ride in on increasingly gusty northwesterly winds. Rain showers will change to snow showers from the top down Monday afternoon. Snow showers taper to flurries by Tuesday morning before ending during the daytime.

Accumulations of a dusting to around an inch will be commonplace for much of the High Country, but with 1-3 inches for western sections. Isolated higher totals will be possible on the western high mountaintops.

Once we get past Tuesday, it will stay chilly for midweek, but with a noticeable moderating temperature trend for Thursday and Friday. After flurries conclude on Tuesday, the rest of the week looks dry.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Latest Insights
Severe Weather Season Around The Corner

As our March weather has become more changeable and volatile, it's a reminder that severe weather season is almost upon us.

Last week was Severe Weather Awareness Week in North Carolina; This week is Virginia's turn.

While western North Carolina and Southwest Virginia may have less overall severe weather than our surrounding neighbors, we still have our fair share, and it's a great time to review your severe weather emergency/preparedeness procedures ahead of time.

Mar 13, 2026
It is, after all, March....

March Madness rules supreme! After record high temperatures on Wednesday, temperatures at noon today are around 40 degrees colder vs. this time on Wednesday.

Oh, and yeah, it snowed in the mountains this morning!

Expect more wild swings this weekend and early next week.

Mar 12, 2026
Monday Message for March 9, 2026

It's March Madness, weather-style, around western North Carolina. We discuss the ups-and-downs of March weather in this week's edition.
